source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Hydraracker

context.region: Gulf of America, Alaminos Canyon Block 337

event.top_event: Worker pinned by Pipe Racking System arm
actions_taken: Emergency Stop system engaged by Assistant Driller
Manual control of PRS arm taken to free injured worker
Worker assisted out of derrick by rig crewmen and medic
Worker flown to UTMB-Galveston for treatment
event.summary: On October 12, 2024, at 0540 hours, a Derrickman (Injured Person/IP) working on the upper fingerboards of the Pipe Racking System during tripping operations was caught and pinned between the upper PRS arm and a stand of pipe. The IP had repositioned himself from the designated safe observation area onto the upper fingerboards without a proper working-at-heights permit to manually secure a malfunctioning fingerboard latch in the open position. While attempting to tie the latch, the PRS initiated an automatic move to retrieve a stand of pipe in the same row, extending the upper arm and pinning the IP. The IP was freed by manual intervention and evacuated for medical treatment.
recommendations: Implement written policies prohibiting personnel from working directly on fingerboards during pipe tripping operations
Incorporate derrick areas into Personnel Zone Management system go/no-go zones
Enforce mandatory working-at-heights permits for any work on fingerboards with moving equipment
Establish active monitoring protocols for CCTV cameras during all tripping operations
Conduct training on hazard recognition and safe-zone compliance for all rig personnel
Address fingerboard latch maintenance and repair procedures to prevent malfunctions

hazards.name: Pipe Racking System operating in automatic mode
Worker positioning in line of fire of moving equipment

threats.name: Failure to implement working-at-heights permit requirement
Lack of written policy prohibiting work on fingerboards during operations
Inadequate situational awareness by multiple parties
Absence of active CCTV monitoring during pipe retrieval
Personnel Zone Management system not incorporating derrick areas

consequences.name: Worker pinned between PRS arm and pipe stand
Evacuation and medical emergency response required

controls.name: Working-at-heights permit requirement
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent unauthorized work at height
controls.lod_basis: Permit system designed to inform all rig workers of hazardous work location and trigger operational shutdown

supporting_text: the IP positioned himself over the handrail located in the derrick, outside of the designated safe-observation area, and onto the upper fingerboards without the proper work permits
the investigation revealed there was no working from heights permit issued during this operation

controls.name: Written policy prohibiting work on fingerboards during operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent unauthorized work on moving equipment
controls.lod_basis: Policy establishes clear prohibition on work directly on fingerboards with moving equipment

supporting_text: BSEE investigation revealed that neither Noble nor LLOG have a written policy addressing working outside of the barricaded walkway in the derrick itself (safe observation area), including policies that specifically prohibit personnel from working directly on the fingerboards during pipe tripping operations

controls.name: Personnel Zone Management system with derrick go/no-go zones
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to establish and communicate safe work zones
controls.lod_basis: Zone management system requires development and posting of go/no-go zones during operations

supporting_text: Noble's Personnel Zone Management system requires the development of go/no-go zones during tripping operations, and a diagram of these go/no-go zones to be posted throughout the rig, at the time of the incident these zones did not incorporate areas in the derrick

controls.name: Closed-Circuit Television (CCTV) monitoring of upper PRS arm
controls.barrier_role: Detection control to identify personnel in hazardous positions
controls.lod_basis: CCTV camera positioned to observe upper PRS arm movements and upper fingerboards during pipe tripping

supporting_text: a Closed-Circuit Television Camera (CCTV) camera was positioned to observe movements of the upper PRS arm during pipe tripping operations, and that the CCTV monitor was active and accessible to all personnel inside the drill shack

the CCTV monitor is only actively monitored during certain portions of tripping pipe, and would not have been actively monitored by either the driller or AD as the PRS arm, in automatic mode, was moving in to retrieve a stand of pipe
none of the 5 personnel in the drill shack saw the IP on the CCTV monitors

controls.name: Visual observation by rig floor crew
controls.barrier_role: Detection control to identify personnel in hazardous positions
controls.lod_basis: Rig floor workers expected to visually observe and communicate hazards

supporting_text: none of the workers on the drill floor visually observe the IP on the fingerboards during tripping operations in the moments leading up to the incident

For the 10 minutes prior to the accident, the rig floor crew members either failed to look up and recognize the hazard, failed to recognize that having a worker on the fingerboards was a hazard, or failed to stop work after recognizing the hazard

controls.name: Emergency Stop system activation
controls.barrier_role: Recovery control to stop equipment and prevent further injury
controls.lod_basis: Emergency Stop system designed to shut off power to all rig floor equipment

supporting_text: The IP began yelling loudly for help which was heard by the coworkers on the rig floor, who immediately communicated to the AD to shut down the PRS. The AD engaged the Emergency Stop system, shutting off power to all rig floor equipment

controls.name: Manual PRS arm control and extraction
controls.barrier_role: Recovery control to manually free trapped worker
controls.lod_basis: Manual control capability allows operator to withdraw equipment and free trapped personnel

supporting_text: The AD took manual control of the PRS and withdrew the upper PRS arm, freeing the IP who was then able to untie himself and egress to the handrail

controls.name: Tie-off equipment for working at height
controls.lod_basis: Tie-off equipment designed to prevent falls and limit worker movement in hazardous areas

supporting_text: Although the IP was observed wearing, and intermittently using, tie off equipment, the investigation revealed there was no working from heights permit issued during this operation
it was observed on the footage that he had untied himself at least once and was moving under the extending PRS arm, less than 5-feet from the edge of the upper fingerboard

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
